name: clickhouse-sqlalchemy

metadata.name: clickhouse-sqlalchemy
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# ClickHouse SQLAlchemy

ClickHouse dialect for SQLAlchemy to [ClickHouse database](https://clickhouse.yandex/).

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/clickhouse-sqlalchemy.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/clickhouse-sqlalchemy
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/xzkostyan/clickhouse-sqlalchemy/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/xzkostyan/clickhouse-sqlalchemy?branch=master
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/clickhouse-sqlalchemy.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/clickhouse-sqlalchemy
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/clickhouse-sqlalchemy.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/clickhouse-sqlalchemy
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/clickhouse-sqlalchemy.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/clickhouse-sqlalchemy
```

```{image} https://github.com/xzkostyan/clickhouse-sqlalchemy/actions/workflows/actions.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/xzkostyan/clickhouse-sqlalchemy/actions/workflows/actions.yml
```

# Documentation

Documentation is available at <https://clickhouse-sqlalchemy.readthedocs.io>.

# Usage

Supported interfaces:

- **native** [recommended] (TCP) via `clickhouse-driver <https://github.com/mymarilyn/clickhouse-driver>`
- **async native** (TCP) via `asynch <https://github.com/long2ice/asynch>`
- **http** via requests

Define table

> ```python
> from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, MetaData
>
> from clickhouse_sqlalchemy import (
>     Table, make_session, get_declarative_base, types, engines
> )
>
> uri = 'clickhouse+native://localhost/default'
>
> engine = create_engine(uri)
> session = make_session(engine)
> metadata = MetaData(bind=engine)
>
> Base = get_declarative_base(metadata=metadata)
>
> class Rate(Base):
>     day = Column(types.Date, primary_key=True)
>     value = Column(types.Int32)
>
>     __table_args__ = (
>         engines.Memory(),
>     )
>
> Rate.__table__.create()
> ```

Insert some data

> ```python
> from datetime import date, timedelta
>
> from sqlalchemy import func
>
> today = date.today()
> rates = [
>     {'day': today - timedelta(i), 'value': 200 - i}
>     for i in range(100)
> ]
> ```

And query inserted data

> ```python
> session.execute(Rate.__table__.insert(), rates)
>
> session.query(func.count(Rate.day)) \
>     .filter(Rate.day > today - timedelta(20)) \
>     .scalar()
> ```

# License

ClickHouse SQLAlchemy is distributed under the [MIT license](http://www.opensource.org/licenses/mit-license.php).

methods.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.types.ip.BaseIPComparator._split_other
documentation:
Split values between addresses and networks
This allows to generate complex filters with both addresses
and networks in the same IN
ie in_('10.0.0.0/24', '192.168.0.1')

methods.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.drivers.compilers.sqlcompiler.ClickHouseSQLCompiler.visit_is_not_distinct_from_binary
documentation:
Implementation of distinctness comparison in ClickHouse SQL.
A distinctness comparison treats NULL as if it is a (singleton)
value and is what ClickHouse uses for `SELECT DISTINCT` and `GROUP BY`.
Some databases have direct support for a `IS DISTINCT` comparison, but
ClickHouse does not, so we rely on the `hasAny` array function here.

classes.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.drivers.native.connector.Error
documentation:
Exception that is the base class of all other error exceptions.
You can use this to catch all errors with one single except statement.

classes.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.drivers.native.connector.Cursor

methods.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.drivers.native.connector.Cursor._reset_state
documentation: Resets query state and get ready for another query.

These objects represent a database cursor, which is used to manage
the context of a fetch operation.

Cursors are not isolated, i.e., any changes done to the database
by a cursor are immediately visible by other cursors or connections.

methods.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.drivers.http.transport.RequestsTransport.execute
documentation:
Query is returning rows and these rows should be parsed or
there is nothing to return.

methods.name: clickhouse_sqlalchemy.drivers.http.transport.RequestsTransport.raw
documentation:
Performs raw query to database. Returns its output
\:param query: Query to execute
\:param params: Additional params should be passed during query.
\:param stream: If flag is true, Http response from ClickHouse will be

> streamed.

return

Convert the given value to an "sample" clause.

This handles incoming element to an expression; if
an expression is already given, it is passed through.
